greet.text: Deane is a hundred and thirty-five and pays a Jensen clinic to keep him that way. He folds soft pink hands on the desk. "Case. Sit. You have the look of a man who lost something and is hoping I found it."

word.text: "Word is cheap, and I am not." The porcelain smile widens. "But for you — someone's been buying up old cowboys' debts. Quietly. If I owed anyone money, I'd be watching the street behind me."

work.text: "There's always work for a man who can think in eight dimensions." He spreads his hands. "Get your deck running and come back. We'll talk numbers over ginger tea."

bye.text: "Do give my regards to Ratz. And try not to die before you're useful to me." He returns to a ledger no one else will ever read.
